review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for reinforcement questions on HFU versus MFU and how to interpret utilization numbers in practice. No separate clip is needed.

question_bank.question: What does MFU measure that HFU does not?
choices: Only raw matmul benchmark efficiency on tensor cores
The fraction of theoretical FLOPs spent on the model's forward/backward work inside the real training loop
The exact network bandwidth between GPUs
Only optimizer-step time with all model compute removed
question_bank.explanation: MFU is stricter because it reflects end-to-end training overheads such as communication, data loading, and recomputation instead of only a clean compute benchmark.

transcript_quotes: So Model Flops Utilization is basically
And that's where we move from Hardware Flops Utilization

question_bank.question: In the rough rule of thumb given here, what MFU range is considered excellent?
question_bank.explanation: The segment treats 30%+ as good and 40%+ as excellent, reflecting how hard it is to keep large distributed systems busy efficiently.

question_bank.question: Why can newer GPUs sometimes show worse MFU even if their peak compute is much higher?
choices: Because newer GPUs cannot run matrix multiplies efficiently
Because compute throughput has improved faster than communication bandwidth, so communication becomes a larger bottleneck
Because MFU ignores forward and backward passes
Because mixed precision is no longer supported
question_bank.explanation: If compute speeds up much faster than the interconnect, the system can spend a larger fraction of time waiting on communication, which hurts achieved utilization.

transcript_quotes: And actually, paradoxically, more recent devices sometimes
You're never going to get really much
